volumeTitle: The Merchant. The Braggart Soldier. The Ghost. The Persian
label: The Braggart Soldier (Section 2.4.363c - Section 2.5.428b)

translation.label: Scaife English translation
translation.citation: Miles Gloriosus, or The Braggart Captain

sections.label: Section 2.4.363c
sections.translation: Die this very instant .

sections.label: Section 2.4.364
sections.original: Vbi iste ést bonus sérvos , qui probri me maxumi innocentem
sections.translation: Where is this faithful servant , who has falsely accused me in my innocence

sections.label: Section 2.4.365
sections.translation: of this most heinous crime ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.365b
sections.original: Em tibi , hic mihi dixit tibi quae dixi .
sections.translation: See , here he is ; ’ tis he that told it me ,— assuredly ’ twas he .

sections.label: Section 2.4.366
sections.original: Tun me vidisse in proxumo hic , sceleste , ais osculantem ?
sections.translation: Villain , did you say that you had seen me next door here kissing ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.367
sections.translation: Besides , he said it was with some strange young man .

sections.label: Section 2.4.367b
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I did . say so , undoubtedly .

sections.label: Section 2.4.368
sections.translation: You , saw me ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.368b
sections.translation: Yes , with these self-same eyes .

sections.label: Section 2.4.368c
sections.translation: I fancy you will lose those eyes , which see more than what they really do see .

sections.label: Section 2.4.369b
sections.translation: By my faith , I shall never be intimidated

sections.label: Section 2.4.370
sections.translation: from having seen what I really did see .

sections.label: Section 2.4.370b
sections.translation: In my foolishness I am delaying too long in parleying with this madman , whom , by the powers , I ’ ll punish with death .

sections.label: Section 2.4.372
sections.original: Noli minitari : scio crucem futuram mihi sepulcrum ;
sections.translation: Forbear to threaten me : I know that the cross will prove my tomb ; there are laid my forefathers , my father , grandfather , great-grandfather , great-great-grandfather . ’ Tis not in possibility , however , for these eyes of mine to be dug out [*] by your threats .

sections.label: Section 2.4.375
sections.original: sed paucis verbis te volo , Palaestrio . opsecro te ,
sections.translation: But I want a few words with you ; prithee , Palaestrio , whence came she hither ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.376b
sections.translation: Whence but from our house ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.376c
sections.translation: From our house ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.376d
sections.translation: Do you credit me [*] ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.376e
sections.translation: I do credit you : but ’ tis a thing to be wondered at , how she has been able to return from that house to ours . For , beyond a doubt , we have neither a terrace to our house , nor any garden , nor any window but what is latticed [*] . ( To PHILOCOMASIUM .) But , undoubtedly , I did see you in the house next door .

sections.label: Section 2.4.380
sections.translation: Do you persist , you rascal , in pretending to accuse her ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.380b
sections.translation: In good sooth , then , the dream has not turned out untrue , that I dreamed last night .

sections.label: Section 2.4.382
sections.translation: What did you dream ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.382b
sections.original: Ego eloquar . sed amabo advortite animum .
sections.translation: I ’ ll tell you ; but , I pray you , give attention . Last night , in my sleep , my twin-sister seemed to have come from Athens to Ephesus with a certain person , her lover .

sections.label: Section 2.4.385
sections.original: ei ambo hóspitio huc in proxumum mihi devortisse visi .
sections.translation: Both of them seemed to me to be having their lodgings here next door .

sections.label: Section 2.4.386
sections.translation: The dream that ’ s being related is Palaestrio ’ s — pray , go on .

sections.label: Section 2.4.387
sections.original: Ego laeta visa , quia soror venisset , propter eandem
sections.translation: I seemed to be delighted because my sister had come , and on her account I seemed to be incurring a most grievous suspicion . For , in my sleep , my own servant seemed to accuse me , as you are now doing ,

sections.label: Section 2.4.390
sections.original: me cum alieno adulescentulo , quasi nunc tu , esse osculatam ,
sections.translation: of being caressed by a strange young man , whereas it was that own twin-sister of mine , who had been toying with her own friend . Thus did I dream that I was wrongfully accused of a crime .

sections.label: Section 2.4.393
sections.original: Satin eadem vigilanti expetunt quae in somnis visa memoras ?
sections.translation: And isn ’ t just the same thing befalling you when awake , that you speak of as seen in your sleep ? Capital ; i ’ faith , the dream is verified : go in-doors , and pray [*] .

sections.label: Section 2.4.395
sections.translation: I should recommend that this be told to the Captain .

sections.label: Section 2.4.395b
sections.translation: I am resolved to do so ; nor , in fact , will I allow myself , with impunity , to be accused of disgraceful conduct . ( Goes into the CAPTAIN ’ S house .)

sections.label: Section 2.4.397
sections.original: Timeo quid rerum gesserim , ita dorsus totus prurit .
sections.translation: I fear for the thing I have done ; my back does so tingle all over .

sections.label: Section 2.4.398
sections.translation: Are you not aware that you are done for ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.398b
sections.original: Nunc quidem domi certo est . certa res est
sections.translation: Now , indeed , I ’ m sure she is at home ; I am now resolved to watch our door , wheresoever she may be . ( Places himself at the door .)

sections.label: Section 2.4.399b
sections.translation: But , prithee , Sceledrus ,

sections.label: Section 2.4.400
sections.original: ut ad id exemplum somnium quam simile somniavit
sections.translation: how very like the dream she dreamt to what has happened ; and how you really did believe that you had seen her kissing . ⋯

sections.label: Section 2.4.402
sections.original: Nescio quid credam egomet mihi iam , ita quod vidisse credo
sections.translation: And do you suppose that I didn ’ t see her ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.403b
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I verily believe you ’ ll come to your senses when ’ tis too late . If this matter should only reach our master , you certainly are undone .

sections.label: Section 2.4.405
sections.original: Nunc demum experior , mi ob oculos caliginem opstitisse .
sections.translation: Now , at length , I find out that there was a mist placed before my eyes .

sections.label: Section 2.4.406
sections.original: Dudum edepol planum est id quidem , quae hic usque fuerit intus .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , that really has been plain for some time now ; as she was here in-doors all the while .

sections.label: Section 2.4.407
sections.original: Nihil habeo certi quid loquar : non vidi eam , etsi vidi .
sections.translation: Not a word of certainty have I to utter ; I did not see her , although I did see her .

sections.label: Section 2.4.408
sections.original: Ne tu edepol stultitia tua nos paene perdidisti :
sections.translation: By my troth , through this folly of yours you certainly have nearly ruined us ; while you have wished to prove yourself faithful to your master , you have been almost undone .

sections.label: Section 2.4.410
sections.original: sed fores vicini proxumi crepuerunt . conticiscam .
sections.translation: But the door of our next neighbour makes a noise ; I ’ ll be silent .

sections.label: Section 2.5.411
sections.original: Inde ignem in aram , ut Ephesiae Dianae laeta laudes
sections.translation: Put fire on the altar , that in my joy I may return praises and thanks to Diana of Ephesus , and that I may send up for her a grateful smoke with odours of Arabia : she who has preserved me in the realms of Neptune and amid the boisterous temples [*] , where with raging billows I have been so recently dismayed .

sections.label: Section 2.5.415
sections.translation: Palaestrio ! O Palaestrio !

sections.label: Section 2.5.415b
sections.translation: Sceledrus ! O Sceledrus ! What is it you want ?

sections.label: Section 2.5.416
sections.original: Haec mulier , quae hinc exit modo , estne erilis concubina
sections.translation: This lady that has come out of that house just now — is she Philocomasium , our master ’ s lady , or is she not ?

sections.label: Section 2.5.417b
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I think , it seems to be she . But ’ tis a wondrous thing how she could pass from our house to next door ; if , indeed , it is she .

sections.label: Section 2.5.419b
sections.original: An dubium tibi est eam esse hanc ?
sections.translation: And have you any doubt that this is she ?

sections.label: Section 2.5.419c
sections.translation: It seems to be she .

sections.label: Section 2.5.420
sections.original: Adeamus , appellemus . heus , quid istúc est , Philocomasium ?
sections.translation: Let us approach her , and accost her . Hallo ! how ’ s this , Philocomasium ? What is there owing to you in that house ? What is your business there ? Why are you silent now ? I am speaking to you .

sections.label: Section 2.5.422b
sections.translation: No , faith , you are talking to yourself ; for nothing at all does she answer .

sections.label: Section 2.5.423b
sections.translation: I am addressing you , woman , brimful of viciousness and disgrace , who are roaming about among your neighbours .

sections.label: Section 2.5.424b
sections.translation: To whom are you talking ?

sections.label: Section 2.5.425
sections.translation: To whom but to yourself ?

sections.label: Section 2.5.425b
sections.original: Quis tu homo es , aut mecum quid est negoti ?
sections.translation: What person are you ? Or what business have you with me ?

sections.label: Section 2.5.426
sections.translation: O , you ask me who I am , do you ?

sections.label: Section 2.5.426b
sections.original: Quin ego hoc rogem quod nesciam ?
sections.translation: Why shouldn ’ t I ask that which I don ’ t know ?

sections.label: Section 2.5.427
sections.original: Quis ego sum igitur , si hunc ignoras ?
sections.translation: Who am I , then , if you don ’ t know him ?

sections.label: Section 2.5.427b
sections.translation: You are an annoyance to me , whoever you are , both you and he .

sections.label: Section 2.5.428b
sections.translation: What ? don ’ t you know us ?
